title: Orecchiette with Sausage & Broccoli
description: A delicious and easy one-pan pasta dish featuring orecchiette, savory sausage, and tender broccoli florets, perfect for a family meal. This recipe is designed to be kid-friendly and minimize cleanup.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add orecchiette pasta and cook according to package directions until al dente. Drain and set aside.

While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Remove sausage from casings (if applicable) and crumble into the skillet. Cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.

Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in broccoli florets and chicken broth. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until broccoli is tender-crisp.
Add the cooked orecchiette pasta to the skillet with the sausage and broccoli. Toss to combine. Stir in grated Parmesan cheese and red pepper flakes (if using).
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan cheese if desired.
tips: For picky eaters, you can finely chop the broccoli before adding it to the skillet to make it less noticeable.
If you prefer a creamier sauce, stir in a splash of heavy cream or a tablespoon of cream cheese at the end.
Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop or in the microwave.
